content.text:
Here are the top recent X posts (sorted by relevance/engagement) matching your search for discussions around GitHub Copilot, Copilot, OpenCode, open-source code tools, or Claude Code in English.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019071652546355544)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018609427117465775)

### Key Highlights:
- **OpenCode as a Terminal-Based AI Coding Alternative**: freeCodeCamp shared a tutorial on OpenCode, an open-source AI assistant that integrates directly into your terminal (unlike browser/editor tools like GitHub Copilot or ChatGPT). It covers installation and benefits.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2019154317400269108)
- **Claude Code Automation Tools**: Multiple posts hype a new open-source tool acting as a "Product Manager" for Claude Code, automating PRDs → Epics → GitHub issues → deployable code. One from @oliviscusAI notes it's 100% open-source.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2017895413160813019)[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2018425788098293866)
- **Other Open-Source Code Releases**:
  - Meta AI's EB-JEPA library: Trainable JEPAs on a single GPU, with GitHub code and arXiv paper.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019071652546355544)
  - SourceRobotics' 3D-printed differential robot arm wrist: Fully open-sourced (STLs, code) on GitHub.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018609427117465775)
  - GitHub's Dependabot Proxy: Now MIT-licensed open-source for supply chain audits (npm, Maven, etc.).[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2018921251142582463)
  - AI-research-SKILLs: Open library packaging AI skills for models like Claude/Codex agents.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2017432645420851253)

These are from the last week (as of Feb 5, 2026). If you want more details on a specific post, latest results, or semantic search refinements, let me know!
